segments.text: Section 10-4 Which Is the Principal Species?

Which Is the Principal Species? (Monoprotic System)
We sometimes must identify which species of acid, base, or intermediate predominates under given conditions.
What is the principle form of benzoic acid at pH = 8.00?

Example: Principal Species—Which One and How Much? (1 of 3)
What is the predominant form of ammonia in a solution at pH 7.0? Approximately what fraction is in this form?

Example: Principal Species—Which One and How Much? (2 of 3)
Solution: In Appendix G, we find p K a = 9.24 for the ammonium ion
the conjugate acid of ammonia, NH 3 ).
At pH = 9.24,
Below pH 9.24,

will be about 100: 1. More than 99% is in the form

Example: Principal Species—Which One and How Much? (3 of 3)
Test Yourself: Approximately what fraction of ammonia is in the form NH 3 at pH 11?
[Speaker Notes] (Answer: Somewhat less than 99% because pH is almost 2 units above pKa)

Which Is the Principal Species? (Diprotic System)
For diprotic systems:
Reasoning is similar.
There are two p K a values.
Oxalic acid (H 2 Ox) has p K 1 = 1.25 and p K 2 = 4.27.
What is the principle species at pH = 3?
What is the principle species at pH = 8?

Which Is the Principal Species? (Triprotic System)
Figure 10-3

Example: Principal Species in a Polyprotic System (1 of 3)
The amino acid arginine has the following forms:
Appendix G tells us that the α -ammonium group (at the left) is more acidic than the substituent (at the right). What is the principal form of arginine at pH 10.0? Approximately what fraction is in this form? What is the second most abundant form at this pH?

Example: Principal Species in a Polyprotic System (2 of 3)
Solution: We know that when pH = p K 2 = 8.99, [H 2 Arg + ] = [ HArg ].
At pH = p K 3 = 12.1, [ HArg ] = [ Arg − ]. At pH = 10.0, the major species is HArg .
Because pH 10.0 is about one pH unit higher than p K 2 , we can say that [ HArg ]/[H 2 Arg + ] ≈ 10: 1. About 90% of arginine is in the form HArg . The second most important species is H 2 Arg + , which makes up about 10% of the arginine.
